briefing.headline: Rig up a Squarebill Crankbait — finesse summer approach for muddy water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Squarebill Crankbait is a strong choice for summer with muddy water and post frontal conditions.

Palaniuk: 'Pick a crankbait that goes deeper than the depth you're fishing, reel at a pace where you keep it in control. When it hits a rock, try to get it to crawl up and over and then back down the other side -- looks like a crawfish slowly scooting, acts like something between a normal crankbait 

Key adjustment for post frontal: slow down significantly — extended pauses, subtle movements, finesse presentations.

primaryApproach.retrieve: Palaniuk: 'Pick a crankbait that goes deeper than the depth you're fishing, reel at a pace where you keep it in control. When it hits a rock, try to get it to crawl up and over and then back down the
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on grass, ledge, hump. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Hackney: 'On a KVD 2.5, replace standard No.2 hooks with short shank No.1 around heavy cover for greater lip-grabbing ability.'
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on grass, ledge, hump. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: Wheeler: When fishing ledges, keep baits on bottom but often burn them fast then kill the retrieve to trigger reaction strikes — this produced key fish at Chickamauga

Hackney: 'Quite a few fish live shallow even during the hottest time of year. They hold in shade of boat docks, overhanging trees, lily pads, and grass. Hot weather triggers grass growth that creates shade, oxygen, and ambush points.'

KVD: In summer on grass lakes, big fish move offshore to the middle of the lake where the bait is. They don't stay near shore when it's hot. Look for scattered hydrilla clumps in the middle of the lake.

Palaniuk: 'Big fish that haven't made the full transition to main summertime structure are singles or doubles on brushpiles or standing timber with a bottom composition change (mud to hard), on straight banks leading from spawning areas to points ...

briefing.depthStrategy: Post-frontal summer fish retreat to reliable offshore structure at 12-25ft. They're less aggressive but still need to eat. Focus on primary structure — main lake points, ledges, and humps with shade or current.
adjustIf: If skies cloud up and wind picks up, re-introduce moving baits — the reprieve from high pressure activates fish
If you find a cleaner pocket or tributary, switch to natural presentations — fish in cleaner water see more lures
If fish are short-striking, add a long pause after deflections to let the lure suspend
